dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4374 IS: Airpower Acceleration Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Budd (for himself, Mrs. Shaheen, Mr. King, Mr. Schmitt, Mr. Sheehy, Mr. Rounds, and Mr. Cramer) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To authorize the Secretary of Defense to enter into multiyear contracts for the procurement of F–35 and F–15EX aircraft, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Airpower Acceleration Act of 2026.

section.header: Multiyear procurement authority for F–35 and F–15EX aircraft

subsection.text: Subject to section 3501 of title 10, United States Code, except as provided in this section, the Secretary of Defense may enter into one or more multiyear contracts for the procurement of F–35A, F–35B, F–35C, and F–15EX aircraft.

paragraph.text: A contract described in subsection (a) is deemed to meet the requirements of section 3501(a) of such title.
Section 3501(i)(2) of such title shall not apply for purposes of this Act.

Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense shall submit to Congress a report with respect to contracts described in subsection (a) containing preliminary findings of the agency head described in paragraphs (1) through (6) of section 3501(a) of such title, together with the basis for such findings.
The Secretary of Defense may enter into one or more contracts for advance procurement of key components of F–35A, F–35B, F–35C, and F–15EX aircraft in economic order quantities.

The Secretary of Defense shall include in any certification submitted under section 3501(i)(3) of such title a description of the specific components the Secretary intends to procure under the authority provided by paragraph (1).

subsection.text: It is the sense of Congress that the implementation of section 3501(d) of such title is important to ensuring that all subcontractors, vendors, and suppliers promptly receive funds in order to supercharge the defense industrial base.

section.header: Modification of inventory requirements for Air Force fighter aircraft

subsection.text: Subsection (i) of section 9062 of title 10, United States Code, is amended to read as follows:

paragraph.text: The Secretary of the Air Force shall—

subparagraph.text: during the period beginning on October 1, 2026, and ending on October 1, 2035, maintain a total aircraft inventory of fighter aircraft of not less than 1,800 aircraft; and
maintain a total aircraft inventory of combat-coded fighter aircraft of—

clause.text: not less than 1,369 aircraft by December 31, 2030; and
not less than 1,558 aircraft by December 31, 2035.

subclause.text: is designated by a mission design series prefix of F– or A–;
is manned by one or two crewmembers; and

executes single-role or multi-role missions, including air-to-air combat, air-to-ground attack, air interdiction, suppression or destruction of enemy air defenses, close air support, strike control and reconnaissance, combat search and rescue support, or airborne forward air control; and

clause.text: does not include collaborative combat aircraft.

subparagraph.text: The term primary mission aircraft inventory means aircraft assigned to meet the primary aircraft authorization to a unit for the performance of its wartime mission.
The term total aircraft inventory of combat-coded fighter aircraft means the total inventory of combat-coded fighter aircraft the Air Force possesses, including in—

subsection.text: The amendment made by subsection (a) shall take effect on October 1, 2026.
Subject to subsection (b), the Secretary of the Air Force may increase the size of the F–15EX aircraft fleet from 129 to 329 F–15EX aircraft.

The Secretary of the Air Force shall use any F–15EX aircraft procured after the first 129 aircraft under the authority provided by subsection (a) exclusively for the purpose of recapitalizing the F–15E aircraft fleet of the Air Force.
